Module com.cuatrifasico.battleshi
Package com.cuatrifasico.battleshi.view.board

Class BoardGridView

java.lang.Object
com.cuatrifasico.battleshi.view.board.BoardGridView
public final class BoardGridView extends Object
Builds and owns the visual grid for a single 10x10 board: the column letters (A-J) and row numbers (1-10), the water cells themselves, and a transparent overlay layer where ships and shot markers are placed.

The logical grid treats every cell as a perfect CELL_SIZE x CELL_SIZE square glued directly to its neighbors â€” cell n starts exactly at n * CELL_SIZE, with no gap baked into that math. The visual "spacing" between water cells is purely cosmetic: each light-blue cell square is drawn inset by half of BoardTheme.CELL_GAP on every side, over the shared dark board background, so two adjacent insets add up to the full visual gap. Ships and shot markers ignore that inset entirely and occupy the full CELL_SIZE square, which is what makes them read as "glued together" pieces with no seams between segments.

This class only builds and exposes the grid; it deliberately knows nothing about game rules or turns, keeping view and controller concerns separate (Single Responsibility / MVC).

  • Constructor Details

    • BoardGridView

      public BoardGridView()
      Builds a new board grid view.

  • Method Details

    • getRootNode

      public javafx.scene.layout.Pane getRootNode()
      Returns:
      The root node to be embedded in the FXML/scene graph.

    • getOverlayLayer

      public javafx.scene.layout.Pane getOverlayLayer()
      Returns:
      The transparent layer where ship and shot marker nodes should be added, already aligned with the cell grid.

    • getCellRectangle

      public javafx.scene.shape.Rectangle getCellRectangle(Coordinate coordinate)
      Parameters:
      coordinate - The cell to look up.
      Returns:
      The background Rectangle representing that cell (the inset light-blue square), useful to attach mouse handlers and hover feedback.

    • getCellOrigin

      public javafx.geometry.Point2D getCellOrigin(Coordinate coordinate)
      Computes the top-left position of a cell's full logical square (ignoring the cosmetic inset), relative to the overlay layer. This is where ship/marker nodes should be translated to, so they occupy the entire cell rather than the inset water sprite.
      Parameters:
      coordinate - The target cell.
      Returns:
      The (x, y) position, in overlay-layer-local coordinates.

    • setOnCellClicked

      public void setOnCellClicked(javafx.event.EventHandler<javafx.scene.input.MouseEvent> handler)
      Attaches a click handler to every cell in the grid. The handler receives the coordinate of the clicked cell via the rectangle's user data, keeping this view decoupled from how the controller reacts to the click (the controller is expected to wrap this in its own mouse event adapter).
      Parameters:
      handler - The click handler to attach to each cell.

    • buildBackground

      private void buildBackground()

    • buildHeaders

      private void buildHeaders()

    • createHeaderLabel

      private javafx.scene.control.Label createHeaderLabel(String text)

    • buildCells

      private void buildCells()

    • createCellRectangle

      private javafx.scene.shape.Rectangle createCellRectangle()

    • buildTurnIndicator

      private void buildTurnIndicator()
      Builds the (initially inactive) border drawn around the 10x10 cell block to signal whose turn it is. It sits outside the grid by BoardTheme.TURN_INDICATOR_MARGIN on every side, and stops short of the coordinate headers rather than surrounding them, per the reference design.

    • setTurnIndicatorActive

      public void setTurnIndicatorActive(boolean active)
      Toggles this board's turn-indicator border on or off. Intended to be called by the controller whenever the active player changes, so it can freely switch the highlight between the player's and the opponent's BoardGridView instances.
      Parameters:
      active - true to show the border, false to hide it.

    • setCellHighlight

      public void setCellHighlight(Coordinate coordinate, boolean valid)
      Marks a cell as a valid or invalid placement target while the player is dragging a ship, by toggling a CSS class rather than setting an explicit fill, so the actual color stays defined in the stylesheet.
      Parameters:
      coordinate - The cell to highlight.
      valid - true to mark it as a valid placement spot.

    • resetCellHighlight

      public void resetCellHighlight(Coordinate coordinate)
      Resets a cell's highlight back to the default water look.
      Parameters:
      coordinate - The cell to reset.
